Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Department of Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development Act (prior review of treaties by Parliament)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Department of Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development Act (prior review of treaties by Parliament)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Department of Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development Act to add certain requirements for the tabling in Parliament of documents relating to treaties entered into by the Minister of Foreign Affairs on behalf of Canada.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Heading.TitleText: Department of Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development Act

Section.Text: The Department of Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development Act is amended by adding the following after section 12:
The following definitions apply in sections 12.2 to 12.5.

Definition.Text: amendment芒聙聜in the case of a treaty, means an official change to a provision of the treaty that affects all the parties to that treaty and that is made under the same terms and conditions as those under which the treaty was created.芒聙聜(amendement)
major treaty芒聙聜includes any treaty that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: requires for its implementation

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the enactment of an Act of Parliament,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the conferment of new powers on His Majesty in Right of Canada, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the imposition of a tax by Parliament;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: imposes a significant direct or conditional financial obligation on Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: concerns the transfer of part of Canada芒聙聶s territory or any change to Canada芒聙聶s boundaries;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: requires Canada to impose immediate or conditional economic or military sanctions on a state;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: concerns the territorial jurisdiction of Canada, including its jurisdiction over a maritime area or airspace;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: concerns international trade or investment or Canada芒聙聶s position in the global economy; or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: concerns Canada芒聙聶s participation in international institutions, including a transfer of jurisdiction to such institutions.芒聙聜(trait脙漏 important)

Definition.Text: Minister芒聙聜means the Minister of Foreign Affairs, except for the purposes of paragraphs 12.2(4)(c) and (5)(b).芒聙聜(ministre)

modification芒聙聜in the case of a treaty, means a change to some of the requirements of the treaty that is negotiated by some of the parties to the treaty 芒聙聰 including Canada 芒聙聰 and that applies only to the mutual relations of those parties, leaving the original provisions to apply to the other parties.芒聙聜(modification)

treaty芒聙聜means an international agreement in writing between a state and Canada that is governed by international law, regardless of whether it is embodied in a single instrument or in two or more related instruments and regardless of its particular designation, and includes a treaty amending a treaty.芒聙聜(trait脙漏)

Subsection.Text: Before ratifying a treaty, in exercising and performing the powers, duties and functions set out in subsection 10(2), the Minister must cause the treaty, together with a memorandum explaining its content and effects, to be tabled in the House of Commons at least 21 sitting days before it is to be ratified.
For the purposes of subsection (1), if the treaty amends a treaty, the Minister must also cause the treaty being amended to be tabled.

Before modifying a treaty that Canada has ratified, in exercising and performing the powers, duties and functions set out in subsection 10(2), the Minister must, at least 21 sitting days before the agreement to modify the treaty is to be concluded, cause to be tabled in the House of Commons

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the treaty being modified;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a memorandum explaining the content and effects of that treaty; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the modification, together with a letter explaining the content and effects of the modification.

Subsection.Text: The explanatory memorandum referred to in subsections (1) and (3) must include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the title of the treaty;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a summary of the treaty;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the name of any minister responsible for drawing up the treaty and the name of any minister responsible for implementing it;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the treaty芒聙聶s implications for Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of Canada芒聙聶s obligations under the treaty;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: an estimate of the expenditures that Canada must incur under the treaty;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the text of any reservation that Canada has made to the treaty or plans to make to the treaty in ratifying it;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a statement indicating whether the treaty allows a state party to it to withdraw from it, denounce it or suspend its operation in respect of another party;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: a record of the consultations on the treaty that Canada held with any interested party other than a foreign government;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: a summary of any Act that Parliament must enact to implement the treaty; and

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: the title of any Act of Parliament that must be amended to implement the treaty.

Subsection.Text: The explanatory letter referred to in subsection (3) must include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a summary of the modification;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the name of any minister responsible for modifying the treaty and the name of any minister responsible for implementing the modification;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the modification芒聙聶s implications for Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of Canada芒聙聶s obligations arising from the modification.

Subsection.Text: In exercising or performing the powers, duties and functions set out in subsection 10(2) in respect of a major treaty that is tabled in the House of Commons under section 12.2, the Minister must obtain the advice of the House of Commons regarding the treaty before ratifying it.

For the purposes of subsection (1), the major treaty is to be reviewed by a committee of the House of Commons designated or established for that purpose, and that committee must submit a report on that review to the House as soon as feasible.
The Governor in Council may, by order, if the Governor in Council considers that exceptional circumstances warrant it, exempt the Minister from the obligations set out in subsections 12.2(1) to (3).

If the Governor in Council makes an order under subsection (1), the Minister must, as soon as feasible after the treaty has been ratified or modified, cause to be tabled in the House of Commons the documents that would have been tabled under section 12.2 but for the exemption granted under subsection (1), together with written reasons for the order.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception 芒聙聰 documents already tabled
Subsection.Text: The Minister is not required to table the documents in the House of Commons if they have already been tabled under section 12.2.

Not later than 21 days after a treaty is ratified or modified, the Minister must cause to be published in the Canada Gazette the treaty and an explanation of any change made to the treaty between its tabling under subsection 12.2(1), (2), or (3) and its ratification or modification, as the case may be.

Not later than seven days after a treaty is ratified or modified, the Minister must publish on the Department芒聙聶s website the treaty and an explanation of any change made to the treaty between its tabling under subsection 12.2(1), (2), or (3) and its ratification or modification, as the case may be.
Not later than three months after a treaty is ratified or modified, the Minister must cause the treaty to be published or modified, as the case may be, in the Canada Treaty Series.
